metadata.scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC students (Delhi)

raw_data.details: The scheme "Post Matric Scholarship for SC Students" by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi, aims to provide financial assistance to SC students pursuing post-matriculation or post-secondary education in recognized institutions within India. The scheme covers maintenance allowance, tuition fees, and additional allowances for specific needs. Two significant benefits include the reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees and monthly maintenance allowances ranging from ₹230 to ₹1,200, depending on the course group. Eligibility criteria include an annual family income limit of ₹2,50,000 and enrollment in a recognized institution. The scheme is implemented by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi.
raw_data.benefits: Maintenance Allowance (per month)Group 1 (e.g., Medicine, Engineering): Hostellers ₹1,200, Day Scholars ₹550Group 2 (e.g., Pharmacy, Nursing): Hostellers ₹820, Day Scholars ₹530Group 3 (e.g., BA/BSc): Hostellers ₹570/, Day Scholars ₹300Group 4 (e.g., ITI, Polytechnic): Hostellers ₹380, Day Scholars ₹230Other BenefitsReimbursement of Compulsory Non-refundable Fees. Study Tour Charges. Thesis Typing/Printing Charges for Research Scholars. Book Allowance for Correspondence Courses. Book Bank Facility for Specified Courses. Additional Allowance for Students With Disabilities. ConditionsBenefits are for the complete duration of the course. Hostel rent is provided as per scheme guidelines.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be a Student. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste. The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹2,50,000. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation/post-secondary course in India. The applicant should not be availing of any other scholarship for the same course from any other source.
raw_data.exclusions: Students availing other scholarships/stipends (e.g., merit scholarships). Students repeating the same stage of education (e.g., B. Com after B. A). Students pursuing two professional courses (exceptions apply post-1980).
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the [e-District Portal](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/) of the Govt. of NCT of Delhi.
Step 2: On the top right corner of the homepage, click “[Login/Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Account/Login)”, then click “[Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/in/en/Account/Register.html)". You will be taken to the "Citizen Registration Form". Carefully read the “Guidelines for Online Registration” provided at the bottom of the page.
Step 3: In the Citizen Registration Form, select "Aadhaar Card" as the Document Type, and provide the Aadhaar Number. Fill in the captcha code and click “Continue”.
Step 4: On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields. If required, verify your Email ID and Mobile Number via OTP. Enter the Captcha code and click “Continue to Register”. You will receive your Login ID and Password on the Mobile Number/Email Address provided during the registration. Application
Step 1: Visit the [e-District Portal](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/) of the Govt. of NCT of Delhi.
Step 2: On the top right corner of the homepage, click “[Login/Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Account/Login)”, you will be taken to the “Citizen Login Form”
Step 3: Provide your Login ID and Password, enter the Captcha code and click “Login”.
Step 4: Go to the home page and click “Apply Service”. Navigate to the scheme that you intend to apply for. Click on the name of the scheme. You will be taken to the scheme's online application form.
Step 5: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions and/or declaration (if any), then click "Finish”.
Step 6: An OTP will be received on the registered mobile number. Provide the OTP and click “Submit”. An acknowledgement of successful submission shall be sent over Email/SMS/Both. Track Application Status
Step 1: Visit the "[Track Your Application](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Public/TrackApplication)" page.
Step 2: Fill in all the mandatory fields. Enter the Captcha Code, and click "Submit". Your current application status will be displayed.
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar CardBank Account Details (Aadhaar-seeded)Previous Year Mark SheetOriginal Fee ReceiptsFee Structure (on institute letterhead with stamp/signature)Income Certificate (issued by SDM, Delhi)Caste Certificate (issued by Govt. of NCT of Delhi)Institution Certificate (confirming no other scholarship availed)Admission/Completion Date ProofDegree/Diploma Certificates (for all prior qualifications)Institute Verification Page (for institutions outside Delhi)

The scheme covers maintenance allowance, tuition fees, and additional allowances for specific needs. Two significant benefits include the reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees and monthly maintenance allowances ranging from ₹230 to ₹1,200, depending on the course group. Eligibility criteria include an annual family income limit of ₹2,50,000 and enrollment in a recognized institution. The scheme is implemented by the Department for the Welfare of SC/ST/OBC, Government of NCT of Delhi. BenefitsMaintenance Allowance (per month)Group 1 (e.g., Medicine, Engineering): Hostellers ₹1,200, Day Scholars ₹550Group 2 (e.g., Pharmacy, Nursing): Hostellers ₹820, Day Scholars ₹530Group 3 (e.g., BA/BSc): Hostellers ₹570/, Day Scholars ₹300Group 4 (e.g., ITI, Polytechnic): Hostellers ₹380, Day Scholars ₹230Other BenefitsReimbursement of Compulsory Non-refundable Fees. Study Tour Charges. Thesis Typing/Printing Charges for Research Scholars. Book Allowance for Correspondence Courses. Book Bank Facility for Specified Courses. Additional Allowance for Students With Disabilities. ConditionsBenefits are for the complete duration of the course. Hostel rent is provided as per scheme guidelines. EligibilityThe applicant should be a Student. The applicant should be from a Scheduled Caste. The annual income of the family of the applicant should not exceed ₹2,50,000. The applicant should be enrolled in a recognized post-matriculation/post-secondary course in India. The applicant should not be availing of any other scholarship for the same course from any other source. ExclusionsStudents availing other scholarships/stipends (e.g., merit scholarships). Students repeating the same stage of education (e.g., B. Com after B. A). Students pursuing two professional courses (exceptions apply post-1980). Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep 1: Visit the [e-District Portal](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/) of the Govt. of NCT of Delhi. Step 2: On the top right corner of the homepage, click “[Login/Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Account/Login)”, then click “[Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/in/en/Account/Register.html)". You will be taken to the "Citizen Registration Form". Carefully read the “Guidelines for Online Registration” provided at the bottom of the page. Step 3: In the Citizen Registration Form, select "Aadhaar Card" as the Document Type, and provide the Aadhaar Number. Fill in the captcha code and click “Continue”. Step 4: On the next page, fill in all the mandatory fields. If required, verify your Email ID and Mobile Number via OTP. Enter the Captcha code and click “Continue to Register”. You will receive your Login ID and Password on the Mobile Number/Email Address provided during the registration. ApplicationStep 1: Visit the [e-District Portal](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/) of the Govt. of NCT of Delhi. Step 2: On the top right corner of the homepage, click “[Login/Register](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Account/Login)”, you will be taken to the “Citizen Login Form”Step 3: Provide your Login ID and Password, enter the Captcha code and click “Login”. Step 4: Go to the home page and click “Apply Service”. Navigate to the scheme that you intend to apply for. Click on the name of the scheme. You will be taken to the scheme's online application form. Step 5: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Acknowledge and agree to the terms and conditions and/or declaration (if any), then click "Finish”. Step 6: An OTP will be received on the registered mobile number. Provide the OTP and click “Submit”. An acknowledgement of successful submission shall be sent over Email/SMS/Both. Track Application StatusStep 1: Visit the "[Track Your Application](https://edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in/Public/TrackApplication)" page. Step 2: Fill in all the mandatory fields. Enter the Captcha Code, and click "Submit". Your current application status will be displayed. Documents RequiredAadhaar CardBank Account Details (Aadhaar-seeded)Previous Year Mark SheetOriginal Fee ReceiptsFee Structure (on institute letterhead with stamp/signature)Income Certificate (issued by SDM, Delhi)Caste Certificate (issued by Govt. of NCT of Delhi)Institution Certificate (confirming no other scholarship availed)Admission/Completion Date ProofDegree/Diploma Certificates (for all prior qualifications)Institute Verification Page (for institutions outside Delhi)Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the maximum annual family income allowed for applicants to be eligible for this scholarship? The family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹2,50,000 per annum, as certified by the Sub-Divisional Magistrate of Delhi. Are students pursuing professional courses like Medicine or Engineering eligible for higher maintenance allowances? Yes, Group I courses like Medicine and Engineering provide ₹1,200 for hostellers and ₹550 for day scholars as monthly maintenance allowance. Do students enrolled in correspondence courses receive any special benefits under this scheme? Correspondence course students are eligible for a book allowance as part of the scholarship provisions. What happens if a student is already receiving another scholarship or stipend? Students availing any other scholarship or stipend, including merit-based awards, are not eligible for this scholarship. Is there any restriction on students repeating the same level of education under this scheme? Students repeating the same stage (e.g., B. Com after B. A) are ineligible, unless progressing to a higher qualification. Are students pursuing dual professional courses eligible for this scholarship? From academic year 1980-81 onwards, studies in two professional courses are permitted under this scheme. What documents are required to prove the applicant's caste eligibility? A valid caste certificate issued by the Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi must be submitted. How are hostel rent charges handled under this scholarship scheme? Hostel rent is reimbursed as per the guidelines specified under the scholarship provisions. Are students studying in institutions outside Delhi eligible to apply? Yes, but they must upload an Institute Verification Page and Parent/Guardian Verification Page on the e-district portal. What is the maintenance allowance for students pursuing Group III courses like BA or BSc? Group III courses provide ₹570 for hostellers and ₹300 for day scholars as monthly maintenance allowance. Is there any allowance provided for students with disabilities under this scheme? Yes, an additional allowance is provided for students with disabilities for the complete duration of their course. What is the process for reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees? Compulsory fees like tuition and library charges are reimbursed upon submission of original fee receipts and fee structure. Are students pursuing vocational courses at the senior secondary level eligible? Yes, vocational courses under Group IV (like ITI or polytechnic diplomas) are eligible with ₹380 for hostellers and ₹230 for day scholars. What proof is required to confirm the applicant's admission in the course? A certificate from the institution specifying admission date, course duration, and confirmation of no other scholarship must be submitted. How are study tour expenses covered under this scholarship scheme? Study tour charges are included in the scholarship benefits and will be reimbursed as per scheme guidelines. What is the book bank facility mentioned under this scholarship scheme? The book bank facility provides access to textbooks for students enrolled in specified courses under the scholarship. Are postgraduate courses like MA or MSc covered under this scholarship? Yes, postgraduate courses like MA/MSc fall under Group II with ₹820 for hostellers and ₹530 for day scholars. What happens if the applicant's bank account is not linked with Aadhaar? The scholarship amount will only be disbursed to bank accounts that are properly seeded with the applicant's Aadhaar number. Sources And References[Guidelines](https://scstwelfare.delhi.gov.in/sites/default/files/scstwelfare/scheme/pms_sc-201718.pdf)
[Website](https://scstwelfare.delhi.gov.in/scstwelfare/services-schemes)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: Post Matric Scholarship for SC students (Delhi)

faqs.question: What is the maximum annual family income allowed for applicants to be eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: The family income of the applicant must not exceed ₹2,50,000 per annum, as certified by the Sub-Divisional Magistrate of Delhi.

faqs.question: Are students pursuing professional courses like Medicine or Engineering eligible for higher maintenance allowances?
faqs.answer: Yes, Group I courses like Medicine and Engineering provide ₹1,200 for hostellers and ₹550 for day scholars as monthly maintenance allowance.

faqs.question: Do students enrolled in correspondence courses receive any special benefits under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Correspondence course students are eligible for a book allowance as part of the scholarship provisions.

faqs.question: What happens if a student is already receiving another scholarship or stipend?
faqs.answer: Students availing any other scholarship or stipend, including merit-based awards, are not eligible for this scholarship.

faqs.question: Is there any restriction on students repeating the same level of education under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Students repeating the same stage (e.g., B. Com after B. A) are ineligible, unless progressing to a higher qualification.

faqs.question: Are students pursuing dual professional courses eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: From academic year 1980-81 onwards, studies in two professional courses are permitted under this scheme.

faqs.question: What documents are required to prove the applicant's caste eligibility?
faqs.answer: A valid caste certificate issued by the Government of National Capital Territory of Delhi must be submitted.

faqs.question: How are hostel rent charges handled under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: Hostel rent is reimbursed as per the guidelines specified under the scholarship provisions.

faqs.question: Are students studying in institutions outside Delhi eligible to apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, but they must upload an Institute Verification Page and Parent/Guardian Verification Page on the e-district portal.

faqs.question: What is the maintenance allowance for students pursuing Group III courses like BA or BSc?
faqs.answer: Group III courses provide ₹570 for hostellers and ₹300 for day scholars as monthly maintenance allowance.

faqs.question: Is there any allowance provided for students with disabilities under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, an additional allowance is provided for students with disabilities for the complete duration of their course.

faqs.question: What is the process for reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees?
faqs.answer: Compulsory fees like tuition and library charges are reimbursed upon submission of original fee receipts and fee structure.

faqs.question: Are students pursuing vocational courses at the senior secondary level eligible?
faqs.answer: Yes, vocational courses under Group IV (like ITI or polytechnic diplomas) are eligible with ₹380 for hostellers and ₹230 for day scholars.

faqs.question: What proof is required to confirm the applicant's admission in the course?
faqs.answer: A certificate from the institution specifying admission date, course duration, and confirmation of no other scholarship must be submitted.

faqs.question: How are study tour expenses covered under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: Study tour charges are included in the scholarship benefits and will be reimbursed as per scheme guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the book bank facility mentioned under this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The book bank facility provides access to textbooks for students enrolled in specified courses under the scholarship.

faqs.question: Are postgraduate courses like MA or MSc covered under this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Yes, postgraduate courses like MA/MSc fall under Group II with ₹820 for hostellers and ₹530 for day scholars.

faqs.question: What happens if the applicant's bank account is not linked with Aadhaar?
faqs.answer: The scholarship amount will only be disbursed to bank accounts that are properly seeded with the applicant's Aadhaar number.
